rationale: The page provides detailed information on the legal framework, procedural aspects, and eligibility criteria for Postal Ballots in India. This is a core topic in Indian Polity and Governance. The bottom half of the page is an advertisement which has been excluded from the cleaned text.
cleanedText:
About Postal Ballot
- Allows voters to cast their votes on an electronically received postal ballot.
- Legal Framework: Governed by Conduct of Election Rules, 1961 and RPA, 1951.
- Postal Ballot embeds 3 exceptions: Polling takes place outside Polling Station, it is by non-EVM methodology, and it takes place prior to the date of poll fixed.
- Issue of ballot paper: A postal ballot paper shall be sent by post under certificate of posting to elector together with a declaration in Form 13A; a cover in Form 13B; a large cover addressed to the returning officer in Form 13C; and instructions for the guidance of the elector in Form 13D.
Postal Ballot Eligibility
- Service Voters: Members of Armed Forces, police forces serving outside their state, and government servants posted abroad.
- Special Voters: President, Vice President, Governors, Cabinet Ministers, and other high-ranking dignitaries and their spouses.
- Absentee Voters: Seniors above 85, PwDs with a benchmark disability certificate (> 40% disability), electors infected with COVID-19, and electors employed in essential services.
- Preventive Detention: Electors who are under preventive detention.
- Election Duty Personnel: Presiding officers, polling officers, polling agents, police personnel, and Returning Officers.
